input.question: We are a student engineering team designing a vehicle for the FSAE competition. Attached is the FSAE rules document. What does rule F.4.1.3 state exactly? Answer with only the text of the rule and no other words.

retrieved_context:
RULE: f.4.1.3
            TITLE: calculations that show equivalence of the structure to one of similar geometry made to meet

            CONTENT:
            rule F.4.1.3: Calculations that show equivalence of the structure to one of similar geometry made to meet
F.4.1.3 Calculations that show equivalence of the structure to one of similar geometry made to meet 
the minimum requirements for a structure made from steel tubing per F.3.2. Equivalency 
calculations must be submitted for energy dissipation, yield and ultimate strengths in bending, 
buckling, and tension.

final_answer: rule F.4.1.3: Calculations that show equivalence of the structure to one of similar geometry made to meet F.4.1.3 Calculations that show equivalence of the structure to one of similar geometry made to meet the minimum requirements for a structure made from steel tubing per F.3.2. Equivalency calculations must be submitted for energy dissipation, yield and ultimate strengths in bending, buckling, and tension.
